insight:
Yesterday was a solid training day! Your 5.4km run at 131 bpm average shows good aerobic base building, and that 46 bpm resting heart rate indicates excellent cardiovascular fitness. With 7.8 hours of sleep and low stress at 27/100, your body handled the session well. The 2,692 calories burned suggests you were quite active beyond just the run.

Your HRV at 118ms is sitting nicely above your baseline range of 91-113ms, which is a green light for today's planned session. Combined with that low resting heart rate and solid sleep, your nervous system is recovered and ready. No need to modify today's workout - your body is telling you it's adapting well to the training load.

Since you burned nearly 400 calories above your 2,300 target yesterday, make sure you're not under-fueling today. Focus on getting those 175g of protein spread throughout the day to support recovery, especially with only 12 days until Chester Half Marathon.
